adds.rationale: Pure-play trapped-ion quantum computing operator; only US-listed quantum pure-play available at this date. IPO'd via SPAC on NYSE October 2021 (listing date 2021-10-01 — pre-as-of, eligible), raised $636M. Fits the profile's named 'quantum' next-wave bucket where the universe of investable pure-plays is otherwise empty. Profile guidance is to invest early in next waves; quantum is pre-commercial today, so this is a small-position high-asymmetry slot, not a core allocation. Replaces AAPL to stay at cap.

news_evidence.headline: IonQ begins trading on NYSE after SPAC merger; raised $636M

removes.rationale: Trim to make room for a quantum pure-play (IONQ). Among the four AI-megacap names in the watchlist (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA), AAPL has the least direct exposure to the generative-AI / accelerated-compute build-out: MSFT carries Azure and enterprise AI distribution, GOOGL carries TPU/search/cloud, NVDA is the accelerator monopoly. AAPL is a mature consumer-hardware franchise — profile guidance 'ride AI wave, trim before crest' applies most cleanly to the megacap with the least incremental AI upside per dollar. Note: not a thesis-negative call on AAPL, just the lowest-marginal-AI name in a capped basket.

self_critique: Considered TSLA (robotics, Aug 19 2021 Optimus reveal at AI Day) and LMT (rockets, Oct 21 2021 LRHW test) as additional adds. Rejected TSLA because at 2021-12-31 Optimus is concept-stage — Musk himself framed it as a 2022 prototype, so the robotics-wave evidence is weaker than IONQ's actual operating-company-on-NYSE evidence; ISRG already provides robotics exposure (surgical robots, a real revenue base). Rejected LMT because RKLB already covers the rockets bucket as a commercial-space pure-play; LMT would be defense-rockets duplicative without a stronger near-term catalyst than the failed October LRHW test. Considered CCJ (uranium operator) but URA ETF already covers the uranium-spot thesis. Considered CEG (Constellation Energy nuclear) but the Exelon spinoff doesn't complete until 2022-02-01 — post-as-of, not eligible. Held to one add / one remove to keep signal-to-noise high; cap-8 is a tight basket and a 3-add/3-remove churn at the first quarter is hard to justify on dated 2021 news alone. No post-date events cited (verified against suppression list: Ukraine invasion 2022-02-24, MSFT-OpenAI 2023-01-23, NUKZ launch 2024-01-24, etc. all excluded). All evidence dated pre-2022-01-01.
